resources.string: SD Maid SE Pro offers better results, more features and options for power users.
SD Maid can\'t connect to Google Play. Is Google Play installed and up to date? Is your Google Account logged in? Try clearing the cache of the Google Play app and rebooting your device.
There was a problem with Google Play. Please try again later or restart your device.\n\nDetails: %s

An internal Google Play error occurred. Please try the following:

• Restart your device
• Clear Google Play Store cache
• Try again later
Unable to connect to Google Play. Please check your internet connection and try again.
Use the \"Restore purchase\" option to restore your purchase. If the issue persists, try clearing Google Play cache and restarting your device.
SD Maid has no ads and doesn\'t sell user data. My work is financed by you ❤️.

• Clean app caches and expendable data
• Remove duplicate files
• Enable scheduled cleanups
• Create custom filters and cleanup rules
• Track storage trends and cleanup history
• More features and controls across the app
• Support continued development

You can choose between a one-time purchase and a cheaper yearly subscription.\nThe subscription starts with a free 14-day trial, after which you\'ll be charged once per year. Trial and subscription are cancellable at any time.\nSame features, just different pricing models.
Prices couldn\'t be loaded right now. Check Google Play and try again in a moment.
Your upgrade is valid across devices on the same Google account.
Play Store synchronization may take time. Try rebooting, clearing Google Play cache or simply wait.
Multiple accounts can confuse Google Play. Log out of other accounts or install through the Google Play website, which forces associations with a specific account.
